goal: Collect 100,000,000 hay as fast as possible on 8x8 farm with single drone

theoretical_minimum.explanation: 100M hay / (25% * 81920 + 75% * 512) = 4,793 harvests needed. Each harvest requires harvest (200 ticks) + move (200 ticks) = 400 ticks. Plus ~120 setup actions (planting 48 companion bushes). Total: (4793*2 + 120) * 200 ticks / 6075 tps = 315.6s. Assumes perfect 25% polyculture rate, zero overhead ticks, and no wasted harvests.

reroll_approach.explanation: 1,221 productive harvests × ~1,004 ticks (harvest + avg 3 re-rolls + move) + 24,000 setup ticks = ~1,249,884 ticks / 6,075 tps = 205.7s

iterations.name: Baseline
iterations.description: Direct copy of hay_basic.py. Simple serpentine sweep with can_harvest check. No watering, no polyculture, no routing optimization.
iterations.notes: Timed out at 3600s with only ~26.2M hay. Baseline rate: ~7,278 hay/sec. Needs ~4x improvement.

iterations.name: Serpentine + Cached SIZE
iterations.description: Cached get_world_size() in variable. Serpentine traversal to eliminate wrap-around moves. Skip can_harvest after first sweep since grass regrows in 0.5s (sweep takes ~4.5s).
iterations.changes_from_previous: Cached SIZE variable, serpentine traversal, removed can_harvest after first pass.
iterations.notes: Still timed out. ~26.4M hay vs 26.2M baseline. Marginal improvement. Fundamental issue: 512 hay/harvest is too slow without polyculture.

iterations.name: Basic Polyculture
iterations.description: For each tile: check companion, plant bush/tree at companion position, harvest with 160x bonus (81,920 hay/harvest). Clean up companion (harvest bush + till twice). Skip carrot companions (need resources we don't have).
iterations.changes_from_previous: Added polyculture companion planting. Goto-based navigation to companion positions. Full cleanup after each polyculture harvest.

iterations.name: Skip Cleanup (FAILED)
iterations.description: Skipped companion cleanup after polyculture harvest. Hypothesis: save ~1200 ticks per harvest. Result: MUCH worse. Bush/tree companions accumulate, permanently destroying grass tiles.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Removed cleanup (harvest+till+till) after polyculture. Left companion plants in place.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. Companion tiles accumulate bushes/trees, destroying grass. Only 4/5 wins. Cleanup is essential.

iterations.name: Efficient Cleanup (till-only)
iterations.description: Replaced harvest+till+till cleanup with just till+till. Skip harvesting the companion bush/tree -- just till twice to restore grassland. Saves 1 action (200 ticks) per polyculture harvest.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Removed companion harvest from cleanup. Just till() twice to restore grass.

iterations.name: Two-Pass Approach (SLOWER)
iterations.description: Pass 1: Scan all tiles, plant companions, store positions in lists. Pass 2: Harvest all tiles. Pass 3: Cleanup using stored positions. Hypothesis: batch planting reduces goto overhead.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Restructured into 3 passes per sweep. Used lists to track companion positions.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. Extra sweep passes + list operations (O(n) append via concatenation) added too much overhead. Three full sweeps + cleanup loop worse than single-pass approach.

iterations.name: Goto-Based Sweep
iterations.description: Replaced move-based serpentine with goto for each tile. After polyculture cleanup, go directly to next sweep tile instead of returning to current tile first. Eliminates 1 goto trip per polyculture harvest.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Goto-based sweep eliminates return trip after cleanup. Saves ~466 ticks per polyculture harvest.
iterations.improvement_vs_baseline: 4.2% faster than iter 5, 10.1% faster than iter 3

iterations.name: Permanent Companion Tiles
iterations.description: Completely different strategy: plant bushes on 56/64 tiles permanently. Keep column 0 (8 tiles) as grass. Tight loop sweep of column 0 only. ~25% automatic polyculture rate with zero plant/cleanup overhead.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Fundamental strategy change. Permanent companion arrangement eliminates all plant/cleanup overhead. 14,656 actions vs 18,328 in iter 7.
iterations.improvement_vs_baseline: 22% faster than iter 7, 30% faster than iter 3

iterations.name: Permanent Companions + Watering
iterations.description: Same as iter 8 but water each tile with 4 tanks per visit. Speeds grass regrowth from 0.5s to ~0.1s, ensuring 100% harvest rate per sweep.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Added use_item(Items.Water, 4) per tile. Eliminates missed harvests from tight timing.
iterations.improvement_vs_baseline: 15% faster than iter 8, 40% faster than iter 3

iterations.name: Two Grass Columns (no water)
iterations.description: 16 grass tiles (columns 0, 4) with 48 companion tiles. No watering. Columns at distance 4 apart don't interfere with each other's companions. Same ~25% poly rate, double harvest count per sweep.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Doubled grass tiles from 8 to 16 in 2 columns. Removed watering.

iterations.name: Two Columns + Water (SLOWER)
iterations.description: Combined 2 columns with watering. Watering overhead on 16 tiles (3,200 extra ticks/sweep) not justified since grass already regrows within sweep time.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. Watering is only useful when sweep time is close to grow time. With 16 tiles, sweep already > 0.5s.

iterations.name: Minimal Single Column
iterations.description: Tightest possible loop: harvest() + move(North) on 8 tiles in column 0. No can_harvest, no water. Cycle = 0.527s > 0.5s grow time. Only 16 actions per sweep (8 harvest + 8 move).
iterations.changes_from_previous: Removed can_harvest check and watering. Single column only. Minimal loop body.
iterations.improvement_vs_baseline: 21% faster than iters 9/10, 53% faster than iter 3

iterations.name: Unrolled Loop + Skip Col 4
iterations.description: Unrolled inner loop (eliminate range() tick). Skip planting on col 4 (distance 4, never contributes to polyculture). Micro-optimizations.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Unrolled inner for loop. Skipped col 4 in setup.
iterations.notes: No meaningful improvement vs iter 12. Within noise (322.3 vs 320.2 with 20 samples each).

iterations.name: Fertilizer Hybrid
iterations.description: Phase 1: use fertilizer for instant growth + WS to cure infection on single tile (33% poly rate). Phase 2: switch to column sweep. Fertilizer supply too limited (~3 uses) to matter.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Added fertilizer phase before column sweep.
iterations.notes: No improvement. Fertilizer accumulation too slow (0.8/s) for meaningful impact. Transition overhead negates gain.

iterations.name: Tight 2-Tile Pair with Water (SLOWER)
iterations.description: 2 grass tiles at distance 4 apart, 62 companion tiles, 33.3% poly rate. Water for fast regrowth. Testing if higher poly rate compensates for higher per-harvest movement cost.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Reduced to 2 grass tiles with maximum poly rate and water.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. 6 actions/harvest vs 2 actions/harvest in iter 12. Higher poly rate (33%) cannot compensate for 3x worse action efficiency.

iterations.name: Companion Re-Roll
iterations.description: Inspired by human_hay.py. Harvest regrowing grass to re-roll companion until it matches Bush at a bush tile. Achieves ~100% polyculture rate with minimal overhead. 81,920 hay per productive harvest.
iterations.changes_from_previous: Companion re-roll loop: repeatedly harvest() + get_companion() until companion is Bush at non-column-0 position. Eliminates passive poly rate dependency.

iterations.name: Small World SIZE 5 (SLOWER)
iterations.description: Re-roll on 5x5 grid to reduce setup cost. Setup drops from ~120 to ~45 actions. But regrowth failures occur when return time falls below 0.5s due to variance.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. Return time 4×1004=4016 ticks (0.66s) looks safe on average but variance causes ~27% of returns to miss regrowth window.

iterations.name: Smallest World SIZE 4 (SLOWER)
iterations.description: Re-roll on 4x4 grid. Return time 3×1004=3012 ticks (0.496s) < 0.5s grow time. Massive regrowth failures.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. Return time systematically under grow time → most harvests miss.

iterations.name: Spaced Tiles P(match)=33% (SLOWER)
iterations.description: 4 grass tiles at (0,0),(0,4),(4,0),(4,4) with all bush companions. P(match) rises to 33% but 4 moves per harvest (800 ticks) outweighs re-roll savings.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. Extra move cost (+600 ticks/harvest) exceeds re-roll savings (-200 ticks/harvest).

iterations.name: Optimized Re-Roll Loop
iterations.description: Nested if checks (avg 2.67 ticks vs 4 per check) + for loop without num_items check for first 1221 harvests. Saves ~8.7K ticks per run.
iterations.notes: Improvement: -1.4s from iter 16 via AST tick reduction.

iterations.name: Mixed Col 0 (6 grass + 2 bush) (SLOWER)
iterations.description: Plant bushes on column 0 rows 6-7 to increase P(match) from 25% to ~28%. Extra 2 skip-moves per cycle and fewer grass tiles cause regrowth issues.
iterations.notes: REGRESSION. Benefits of higher P(match) wiped out by cycle overhead and regrowth risk.

iterations.name: Tuple Unpack Optimization
iterations.description: Avoid Subscript AST cost by using tuple unpacking: companion, (cx, cy) = get_companion(). 'if cx:' costs 1 tick vs 'if comp_pos[0]:' at 2 ticks. Saves ~1,625 ticks/run.
iterations.improvement_vs_baseline: 35% faster than iter 12, 1.7s faster than iter 16
